description.p: Kashot is a fast, free, tray-resident screenshot tool with a built-in annotation editor. Hit a global hotkey, drag a region, annotate, and save / copy / pin to screen.

ul.li: Global hotkey opens a region selector across all monitors
Nine annotation tools: pen, line, arrow, rectangle, ellipse, marker, text, numbered step, blur/pixelate
Save, copy to clipboard, or pin to screen as an always-on-top window

screenshot.caption: Overlay editor with region selector and annotation tool palette
Tray-resident with one-click capture, delay capture, and record submenus
Saved screenshot with the optional Kashot watermark applied

description.p: Linux in-app updater: the Updates dialog's Download button now downloads the new release, verifies its SHA-256, swaps the running binary in place, and relaunches — the same one-click upgrade Windows MSI installs already had, instead of just opening a browser. The download action is disabled when you're already on the latest version. The binary swap is cross-device-safe (handles /tmp and the install dir living on different filesystems).

UI rendering fixes across the About, Settings, Updates, and Convert dialogs — non-ASCII characters in the bitmap font (em-dashes, middle dots, ellipses, the copyright symbol) were rendering as question marks. The Updates dialog's changelog bullets in particular were showing as "???" per line. Now uses ASCII alternatives throughout and the markdown parser walks proper UTF-8 boundaries. Windows MSI installer adds a "Launch Kashot" checkbox on the final wizard page (checked by default) so the app auto-starts after a fresh install or in-app update — no more closed-installer-with-no-Kashot moment.

Windows fix: clicking the tray "Capture" item could leave the tray context menu (or the hidden-icons overflow flyout, if the user reached the icon via the chevron) painted on screen when the screenshot fired, so the menu ended up in the capture. Now explicitly dismisses both Win32 surfaces and waits long enough for the fade-out animation to finish before xcap shoots the desktop. Other platforms unaffected.

Snap-only fix: declares the `audio-playback` plug alongside `audio-record` to satisfy the Snap Store policy gate. v0.3.5's snap upload was rejected at processing time even though the binary itself was fine. No app-level changes from v0.3.5; existing installs across other channels do not need to update.

CI fix only: snap publish now calls `snapcraft upload` directly with the env-var-based auth that snapcraft 8.x emits, instead of the legacy `snapcore/action-publish@v1` which only accepts the old Ubuntu One credential format. Unblocks the snap-store publishing path. No app-level changes from v0.3.4.

Windows mic recording: when no devices are found, the error now leads with the Windows Privacy fix (the actual root cause 95% of the time) instead of telling users to "plug in a microphone". CI: snap publish step now resolves the kashot_*.snap filename in shell so the upload-to-edge job works on tag pushes.

Windows MSI update flow now shows the full installer wizard (license, install dir, confirm) instead of a silent progress bar, and asks the user to confirm before closing the app. Save-your-work prompt makes the close-and-reopen feel intentional rather than abrupt.

Windows audio recording overhaul: smart device picker (prefers Microphone-prefixed devices; recognizes Stereo Mix / VoiceMeeter / VB-Audio for system audio); pre-flight permission probe surfaces actionable errors for the Windows 10/11 Microphone privacy block, exclusive-mode lock, and device-disappearance cases instead of silently producing a muted MP4.

Windows MSI now bundles ffmpeg.exe (recording works on fresh installs); MSI-aware in-app self-updater (Updates dialog downloads Kashot.msi and runs msiexec MajorUpgrade when running from an MSI install); zip stager hard-fails when ffmpeg is missing.
Windows screen recording; marker opacity slider; hotkey rebind widget; Linux arm64 release; Apache-2.0 license; C# build retired.
